data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Mass migrations and raids by the Sea Peoples disrupted trade, political stability, and military defense across the Eastern Mediterranean, precipitating collapse.", "mechanism": "Coordinated external pressure from maritime raiders overwhelmed local militaries and broke inter‑regional trade networks, leading to internal collapse.", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city‑states"], "excludes": ["Egypt (which repelled the Sea Peoples)"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Archaeological layers at Ugarit lacking any destruction horizon or Sea Peoples artefacts dated to 1200–1150 BC", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "This is the classic ‘Sea Peoples’ hypothesis favored by many scholars; the counterexample of sites with no destruction evidence challenges its universality."}
